text: The surgeon uses cold scissors for sharp dissection to navigate and remove dense adhesions, gradually revealing the location of the gallbladder amidst the challenging adhesions.
The surgeon uses suction to assist in dissection, minimizing trauma to the surrounding tissues, and begins the posterior dissection of the gallbladder.
The surgeon identifies an unusually long and wide dilated cystic duct, initially mistaken for the common bile duct (CBD). The cystic duct is looped twice, and bile is visible within the duct.
The surgeon gently teases the gallbladder away from the liver bed, carefully separating it from the surrounding liver tissue.

The surgeon encounters dense adhesions forming a pseudomembrane across the liver, obscuring the liver behind these adhesions. The XLC duct of Dushka is identified, and clips are applied to manage these structures.
The surgeon completes the cholecystectomy procedure, with the final visual checks ensuring all surgical steps have been performed correctly.
